_comment: Bugfix narrative deck for slidey-128 (grid cards narration drift). Every number, diff, and test count is replayed verbatim from the real bugfix run recorded in stories/slidey-bugfix/cassettes/tour.cassette.yaml (slidey main commit 19c8798). Scene types match stories/slidey-edit/baked/deck.json so it bakes to static HTML via `slidey bundle`.

scenes.title: Grid cards drift out of sync
scenes.subtitle: Narration runs ahead of the cards past the sixth item
scenes.narration: On a grid cards scene with more than six items, the narration runs ahead of the on-screen reveals. We drove the whole fix through the kitsoki bugfix pipeline — reproduce, implement, test, validate — unsupervised.

scenes.lede: From the seventh card, the audio slips.
scenes.body: A grid `cards` scene with eight items and per-item narration plays in lock-step for the first six cards, then drifts. By the last card the spoken line is roughly two-thirds of a second ahead of the reveal — visible and audible. The narration scheduler trusts the frame estimate, and the estimate had quietly diverged from what the renderer actually holds.
scenes.narration: A grid with eight items plays in sync for six cards, then drifts. By the last card the narration is about two-thirds of a second ahead. The scheduler trusts the estimate, and the estimate was wrong.

scenes.title: What broke
scenes.narration: Two functions that must agree disagreed past the sixth card: the estimator counted thirty frames per overflow item, the renderer held twenty.

scenes.title: Reproduction — RED before the fix
scenes.caption: A deterministic unit test in test/timing.test.js computes the renderer-true total for an 8-item grid and asserts estimateScene equals it. It failed: expected 464, actual 484.

scenes.lede: One source of truth, two fallbacks.
scenes.body: timing.js carries a NOTE that the estimator and renderer must stay in lock-step, because narration audio is laid against the estimated boundaries. The TIMING table only defines cards_item_0 through cards_item_5. For every card beyond the sixth, the estimator used a 30-frame fallback while the renderer's setState used its generic 20-frame fallback — so the estimate ran ten frames long per overflow card.
scenes.narration: The estimator and renderer must stay in lock-step — but the table only keys the first six items, and the two functions used different fallbacks past that point. Ten frames long per extra card.
The fix is one fallback: thirty becomes twenty, matching the renderer, with a comment pinning the contract. In-table grids and every other scene type are untouched.

scenes.title: Validation — GREEN after the fix
scenes.caption: no build step — timing.js is pure CommonJS, exercised directly by node --test. The regression test pins the estimate to the renderer hold for 6-, 8-, and 12-item grids.

scenes.body: The pipeline reproduced the bug with a failing test, applied the minimal fix, ran the regression green, and validated the full suite — then parked on a clean branch carrying the summary, the diff, and the test log. Merged to slidey main as commit 19c8798.
scenes.narration: Reproduced with a failing test, fixed minimally, validated green, and parked PR-ready on a clean branch. Merged as commit 19c8798.
